title: Micromeria fruticulosa
intro: Micromeria fruticulosa, also known as Creeping Thyme, is a low-growing, woody-stemmed perennial plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help in the treatment of various health conditions such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Micromeria fruticulosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Micromeria fruticulosa is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of essential oils and topical creams.
taxonomy: Micromeria fruticulosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Micromeria and is closely related to other species within the same genus. Micromeria fruticulosa is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, rocky areas. The plant is classified as a shrubby perennial herb, characterized by its small leaves and purple flowers. Within the plant kingdom, Micromeria fruticulosa is classified as a member of the class Magnoliopsida, order Lamiales, and family Lamiaceae.
properties: Micromeria fruticulosa is a plant species found in the Mediterranean region, and it has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its therapeutic properties. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Studies have suggested that Micromeria fruticulosa may have potential in treating various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. The plant's extracts have also been found to exhibit anti-cancer properties and may have a protective effect on the cardiovascular system. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Micromeria fruticulosa and its applications in modern medicine.

preparations: Micromeria fruticulosa is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations made from its leaves are believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves are typically dried and then used to make teas, infusions, or extracts.
